tools.name: http_session_open
tools.description: Open a reusable HTTP session. Returns a session_id to pass into http_session_request. Optional base_url is prefixed onto relative request URLs; optional default_headers are merged into every request (per-call headers win on conflict). Sessions persist to <workdir>/.cache/stado-http-session/<id>.json so they survive across tool calls and stado restarts.
tools.schema: {"type":"object","properties":{"base_url":{"type":"string","description":"e.g. https://api.example.com — prefix for relative URLs"},"default_headers":{"type":"object","additionalProperties":{"type":"string"},"description":"e.g. {\"Authorization\":\"Bearer ...\"}"},"session_id":{"type":"string","description":"Optional; supply to re-open or rename an existing session. Must match [A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,64}."}}}

tools.name: http_session_request
tools.description: Issue an HTTP request inside an open session. Cookies harvested from prior responses are re-emitted on subsequent calls to the same host; default headers from open() are merged in. URL may be absolute or relative-to-session-base_url. Body is base64-encoded for binary safety. Returns the same shape as stado_http_request plus the post-response cookie jar for that host.

tools.name: http_session_close
tools.description: Discard a session's cached state (cookie jar, default headers). The on-disk file is blanked (no fs_unlink host import yet) so the slot is reusable.
